← All good news Science Wins

A Short Course of Immunotherapy Left Bowel Cancer Patients Cancer-Free for Nearly Three Years

In a UK trial, a few weeks of treatment before surgery worked so well that some patients didn't need surgery at all.

via Science Daily · 9 hours ago

🔬

In the NEOPRISM-CRC trial led by researchers at UCL and UCLH, 32 patients with a specific genetic subtype of stage two or three colorectal cancer received up to nine weeks of the immunotherapy drug pembrolizumab before surgery, instead of chemotherapy afterward.

The results were striking: patients have stayed cancer-free for nearly three years, and some responded so completely that surgery turned out to be unnecessary — a glimpse of a gentler, more effective way to treat the disease.

Read the full story at Science Daily →

How Cloudbreak works

We comb publishers across the web for the stories that make your day a little brighter, show you the headline and a quick summary, then send you straight to the original source for the full story. We don't republish anyone's work — good news belongs to everyone, and credit belongs to the people who reported it.

Spotted something good?

A neighbor, a rescue, a small win on your street — if it made you smile, send it our way.
